name: mc-dp-icons
description: A polished, Minecraft-inspired icon suite for the modern developer's workflow.

mc-dp-icons.enableWorkspaceDetection.title: Enable Workspace Detection
mc-dp-icons.enableWorkspaceDetection.markdownDescription:
If enabled, the extension scans your workspace for `pack.mcmeta` and automatically applies the `mc-dp-icons` theme if detected. If not, it switches back to your `#mc-dp-icons.fallbackIconTheme#`.

This scan runs on startup and when modifying files.

mc-dp-icons.fallbackIconTheme.title: Fallback Icon Theme
mc-dp-icons.fallbackIconTheme.markdownDescription:
*Requires `#mc-dp-icons.enableWorkspaceDetection#` to be enabled.*

Enter the exact ID of the icon theme you want to use for non-Minecraft workspaces (e.g., `vs-seti` or `material-icon-theme`).

If blank, the extension defaults to the icon theme that was active before `mc-dp-icons` was applied.

mc-dp-icons.enableNamespaceIcons.title: Changes namespace folders icons to an ender chest
mc-dp-icons.enableNamespaceIcons.markdownDescription:
If enabled, namespace folders (folders directly within `data` and `assets`) will change their icons to ender chests. Works only if there is `pack.mcmeta` in the same directory.

*Warning: this feature is not currently supported for VS Code web.*

mc-dp-icons.enableOverlayIcons.title: Changes overlay folders icons to glass chest

mc-dp-icons.enableSubfolderIcons.title: Enable subfolder icons
mc-dp-icons.enableSubfolderIcons.markdownDescription:
If enabled, json files inside namespace subfolders will also change their icons (e.g. `data/namespace/item_modifier/some_folder/file.json` will change its icon to a diamond pickaxe when this is enabled). Not recommended for large workspaces. Works only if there is `pack.mcmeta` in the same directory.

*Warning: this feature is not currently supported for VS Code web.*

mc-dp-icons.enableChristmasIcons.title: Use Christmas icons
mc-dp-icons.enableChristmasIcons.markdownDescription:
Choose when to use Christmas icons.

*Warning: this feature is not currently supported for VS Code web.*

mc-dp-icons.hideFolderArrows.title: Hide folder arrows
mc-dp-icons.hideFolderArrows.markdownDescription:
If enabled, the extension will hide folder arrows in the explorer.

*Warning: this feature is not currently supported for VS Code web.*

mc-dp-icons.enableLoadTickAutoChange.title: Dynamic icons for tick and load functions
mc-dp-icons.enableLoadTickAutoChange.markdownDescription:
If enabled, load and tick functions will change their icons to repeating and impulse command blocks accordingly, based on values provided in `tick.json` and `load.json` files found in the workspace.

If not enabled, load and tick icons will be set by their names, that are set by the user in the settings below.

*Warning: this feature is not currently supported for VS Code web.*

mc-dp-icons.functionNamesForTick.title: Tick function names
mc-dp-icons.functionNamesForTick.markdownDescription:
**Only works if `#mc-dp-icons.enableLoadTickAutoChange#` is NOT enabled**. Put here all the names of function files (without `.mcfunction`) you'd like to have a repeating command block icon.

*Warning: this feature is not currently supported for VS Code web.*

mc-dp-icons.functionNamesForLoad.title: Load function names
mc-dp-icons.functionNamesForLoad.markdownDescription:
**Only works if `#mc-dp-icons.enableLoadTickAutoChange#` is NOT enabled**. Put here all the names of function files (without `.mcfunction`) you'd like to have an impulse command block icon.

*Warning: this feature is not currently supported for VS Code web.*

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
